div.head: The Ambassador in China (Johnson) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 712. Under instructions from the Military Attaché, Captain RobertsCapt. Frank N. Roberts, U. S. A., language officer in China. asked the General Staff to obtain permission from the Field Commander at the Shanghai Front for Captain SutherlandCapt. Edwin M. Sutherland, U. S. A., language officer in China. to visit Shanghai hostilities as a military observer. Field Commander refused. On September 13 I wrote informally to General Chiang strongly urging reconsideration. I received no reply even though I indirectly brought influence to bear. On September 23 I brought the matter again to the attention of General Chiang through DonaldW. H. Donald, Australian adviser to Generalissimo Chiang Kai-shek. but latter informed me by letter that Chiang replied Chinese officers at the front could not assume risk of possible injuries to a foreign officer and refusal was definite. Unless Department instructs otherwise I shall not renew attempt.
